items.name: Imposter Syndrome
items.description: Feeling like you don't deserve your role despite evidence of competence
items.symptoms: Attributing success to luck, fear of being 'found out,' avoiding challenges
items.common_triggers: New job, code review feedback, comparing to senior devs
items.intervention: Track your accomplishments, seek mentorship, remember everyone feels this way

items.name: Always-On Stress
items.description: Inability to disconnect from work due to Slack, email, and on-call
items.symptoms: Checking Slack on weekends, anxiety when phone buzzes, working during dinner
items.common_triggers: On-call rotations, remote work blurring boundaries, FOMO on messages
items.intervention: Set communication boundaries, use Do Not Disturb, schedule 'off' hours

items.name: Technical Overwhelm
items.description: Feeling behind on technologies, frameworks, and industry trends
items.symptoms: Tutorial hell, anxiety about new frameworks, feeling outdated
items.common_triggers: Seeing new tech announcements, LinkedIn posts, job postings listing unfamiliar tools
items.intervention: Focus on fundamentals (they transfer), ignore hype cycles, learn one thing deeply

items.name: Burnout
items.description: Chronic workplace stress characterized by exhaustion, cynicism, and reduced efficacy
items.symptoms: Dreading work, emotional numbness, declining performance, physical exhaustion
items.common_triggers: Prolonged overwork, lack of autonomy, misaligned values, toxic environment
items.intervention: Take time off, address root causes, set boundaries, consider role change if needed

items.name: Deadline Pressure
items.symptoms: Racing heart, difficulty sleeping before deadline, rushing through code
items.common_triggers: Sprint deadlines, launch dates, client commitments
items.intervention: Break work into smaller milestones, communicate early about delays, prioritize ruthlessly

comparisons.insight: Not all stress is the same. Imposter syndrome requires mindset work. Always-on stress requires boundary-setting. Technical overwhelm requires focus on fundamentals. Burnout requires systemic change. Identify your stress TYPE before choosing an intervention.

keyInsight: Developer stress comes from specific sources (imposter syndrome, always-on culture, technical overwhelm, burnout, deadline pressure). Each requires a different intervention. Before trying stress reduction techniques, identify WHERE your stress comes from.
